beginnerGuide.title: Block Tales Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the core Block Tales loop: explore chapters, fight turn-based battles, level up, and build your card loadout.
beginnerGuide.intro: Block Tales is a Roblox turn-based RPG inspired by classic RPG combat and chapter-based progression. New players should first understand HP, SP, BP, cards, blocking, and the early route from the Prologue into Chapter One.

steps.title: Start with the Prologue
steps.goal: Learn the story setup and basic controls.
steps.details: The main Block Tales story begins after the Player is sent back to 2010, wakes up in Tutorial Terry's room, and is directed toward Roblox HQ. The Prologue teaches real combat, dashing, leveling, the Wooden Sword, and the first card tutorial.
steps.beginnerTip: Follow the story prompts before grinding; early mechanics are introduced through the route itself.

steps.title: Understand HP, SP, and BP
steps.goal: Know what each main stat controls before choosing level-up rewards.
steps.details: HP is the Player's health and starts at 10. SP is spent on card abilities in battle, while BP controls how many cards can be equipped.
steps.beginnerTip: Pick HP when survival feels tight, SP when you use active cards often, and BP when your deck needs more room.

steps.title: Use Cards as Your Build System
steps.goal: Equip cards to change attacks, utility, defense, and strategy.
steps.details: Cards can be earned from characters, locations, shops, and enemy drops. Many cards require BP to equip, and active cards often spend SP when used in combat.
steps.beginnerTip: Early Block Tales players should keep a simple deck: one main attack card, one defensive option, and one utility card.

steps.title: Practice Turn-Based Combat
steps.goal: Win fights by timing attacks, blocking, and using the right action.
steps.details: Combat uses turn actions for attacking, defending, helping allies, and using cards. HP and SP dials sit at the bottom of the battle screen, and blocking reduces incoming damage when timed well.
steps.beginnerTip: Treat blocking as a core Block Tales skill; it saves more resources than repeatedly healing.

steps.title: Follow the Chapter One Route
steps.goal: Move from the Prologue into the first full chapter without getting lost.
steps.details: After Noobador, the Player enters the caves, learns more ball interactions, reaches Roadtown, searches for Accountant Jim, uses Dynamite, and pushes toward Blackrock Castle.
steps.beginnerTip: When a path looks blocked, review your latest field tool or card; early obstacles usually teach a new interaction.

steps.title: Level Up with a Plan
steps.goal: Use each 100 XP level-up choice to support your playstyle.
steps.details: A level-up consumes 100 XP and lets players increase HP by 5, SP by 5, or BP by 3. The upgraded stat is restored to its new limit after the choice.
steps.beginnerTip: Balanced early Block Tales progression works well: take HP for safety, then BP once you have enough useful cards to equip.

demo5Guide.title: Block Tales Demo 5 Guide
demo5Guide.subtitle: A player-facing overview of Demo 5, Chapter Five, Trouble on the Heights, Windforce, and the rewrite update.
demo5Guide.intro: Demo 5 is the April 25, 2026 Block Tales update that adds Chapter Five and a broad rewrite pass. This module helps returning players quickly see what changed, what to play first, and which new systems deserve attention.

items.title: Demo 5 - Chapter 5 + Rewrite Release
items.summary: Block Tales received Demo 5 with Chapter Five and the rewrite release.

items.title: Chapter Five: Trouble on the Heights
items.summary: Chapter Five continues the main chapter structure and centers the new route toward the Heights and Windforce.
highlights: New Chapter Five intro and ending assets

items.title: New Playable Content
items.summary: The update adds major new progression content for returning Block Tales players.

items.title: Quality-of-Life Additions
items.summary: Demo 5 improves save, inventory, and avatar personalization systems.

items.title: Story Rewrite Changes
items.summary: The rewrite expands story presentation and adjusts earlier scenes.

items.title: Combat and Card Adjustments
items.summary: The update changes several combat interactions and card-related systems.
highlights: Slingshot and Dynamite receive a new action command

walkthrough.title: Block Tales Walkthrough
walkthrough.subtitle: Follow the main Block Tales route from the Pre-Prologue and Prologue through Chapter Five.
walkthrough.intro: This walkthrough module organizes Block Tales by story chapter so players can jump to the exact point where they are stuck. Each chapter card focuses on the main objective, important areas, key unlocks, and major fights.

chapters.title: Attend VIP
chapters.mainObjective: Reach the Roblox VIP Convention, learn the story setup, and trigger the time-travel incident.
chapters.routeTip: Use this Block Tales segment as the story introduction before the main 2010 adventure begins.

chapters.title: Start the 2010 Adventure
chapters.mainObjective: Wake up in Tutorial Terry's room, reach Roblox HQ, rescue Shedletsky, and pursue Red Noob, Blue Noob, and Noobador.
chapters.routeTip: Break wooden planks with the Wooden Sword and treat Power Stab as your first real damage upgrade.

chapters.title: Retrieve the Ice Dagger
chapters.mainObjective: Travel from the caves to Roadtown, rescue Accountant Jim, enter Blackrock Castle, and defeat the Cruel King.
chapters.routeTip: When the castle path is blocked, look for puzzle routes through the basement, attic, armory, diner, and kitchen.

chapters.title: Chase the Venomshank
chapters.mainObjective: Fly to Plainstown, travel through the savannah and rainforest, chase Griefer, and finish the Venomshank chapter.
chapters.routeTip: Rocket Boots and the Iron Sword both solve route blockers, so revisit split paths after each unlock.

chapters.title: Enter Telamon's Manor
chapters.mainObjective: Take the train to Spooksville, reach Telamon's Manor, prepare the Ghost Potion, enter the catacombs, and confront the Ghostwalker route.
chapters.routeTip: The manor route is puzzle-heavy; keep track of cooking ingredients, ghost interactions, and Hyperball floor breaks.

chapters.title: Seek the Firebrand
chapters.mainObjective: Cross the desert route, work through the Ancient Tomb and Red Sun temple path, then complete the Firebrand chapter.
chapters.routeTip: Expect more environmental puzzles in this Block Tales chapter, including ship, sand, torch, light, and mountain traversal sections.

chapters.chapter: Chapter Five: Trouble on the Heights
chapters.title: Play the Demo 5 Route
chapters.mainObjective: Continue from Chapter Four into the new Demo 5 chapter and push toward the Heights and Windforce.
keyAreas: Sword Fight on the Heights route
chapters.routeTip: Use the Block Tales Demo 5 module first for update changes, then follow the chapter route card for exact progression.

cardsGuide.title: Block Tales Cards Guide
cardsGuide.subtitle: Browse the core Block Tales card system by card type, BP cost, SP cost, unlock source, and combat role. Use it to plan early-game decks, boss builds, support builds, and The Pit loadouts.
cardsGuide.intro: Cards are the main build system in Block Tales. They can add new battle actions, upgrade weapons, improve defense, generate SP, support allies, change field movement, or increase difficulty for extra challenge. Most cards cost Build Points to equip, and BP can be refunded by removing cards from a build.

items.name: Build Points
items.stacking: Level-up BP choices increase maximum BP by 3 until the 30 BP cap.
items.how_to_get: Choose BP when leveling up; removing equipped cards refunds the BP they cost.
items.combat_role: Controls how many cards can fit into one build and is the main limit on card combinations.

items.name: Action Cards
items.stacking: Many Action Cards can stack to improve effects or change SP cost.
items.how_to_get: Found in locations, bought from card sellers, received from characters, or dropped by enemies.
items.combat_role: Adds selectable combat moves such as healing, status effects, support, or stronger attacks.

items.name: Passive Cards
items.stacking: Some passives stack; others are single-copy build-defining cards.
items.how_to_get: Found, bought, unlocked from boss rewards, or earned from special encounters.
items.combat_role: Changes stats, SP economy, damage output, guard strength, item value, or battle-start effects.

items.name: Ball
items.how_to_get: Received from JParty.
items.combat_role: Ranged attack that can choose targets, hit Flying enemies, and use the action command second hit to ignore defense.

items.name: Sword
items.how_to_get: Received from Shedletsky after defeating Red Noob and Blue Noob.
items.combat_role: Melee attack that pierces part of enemy defense and becomes the base for Sword-slot attack cards.

items.name: Daze
items.how_to_get: Found on Pit Floor 10 and in Splintered Skies, or bought from Sketchy Figure and Wealthy Merchant.
items.combat_role: Applies Dizzy and deals splash damage to the enemy behind the target, making it valuable against dangerous attackers and grouped enemies.

items.name: Minimize
items.how_to_get: Bought from Desna or Gene, and dropped by several noob enemies.
items.combat_role: Applies Small for several turns, lowering threat from enemies that rely on direct attacks.

items.name: Charge
items.how_to_get: Bought from Wealthy Merchant or Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Adds +2 attack to the next hit, useful for burst turns, single-target boss damage, and stacked damage builds.

items.name: Charge DEF
items.how_to_get: Bought from Gene or Banished Knight.
items.combat_role: Grants Halved Damage for three turns, helping survive boss burst windows and Hard Mode encounters.

items.name: Cure
items.how_to_get: Bought from Desna or Tai.
items.combat_role: Heals a target for 5 HP, trading offensive power for reliable recovery in long fights and multiplayer support.

items.name: Feel Fine
items.how_to_get: Found in Outer Scorched Dunes, bought from Gene or Tai, and dropped by status-themed enemies.
items.combat_role: Cures and prevents status ailments for several turns, especially useful in fights with Burn, Dizzy, Freeze, Poison, or other control effects.

items.name: Bodyguard
items.how_to_get: Bought from Wealthy Merchant or Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Redirects part of an ally's incoming enemy damage to the user for three turns, making it a strong multiplayer protection tool.

items.name: Good Vibes
items.how_to_get: Bought from Sketchy Figure or Amber.
items.combat_role: Puts the user to sleep while restoring HP and SP to the party each turn, turning one player into a sustain engine.

items.name: Resurrect
items.how_to_get: Bought from Sketchy Figure or Tai.
items.combat_role: Revives a downed ally with 5 HP and can also trigger a self-revive once per battle when enough SP is available.

items.name: Sacrifice
items.how_to_get: Bought from Banished Knight or Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Spends the user's HP to heal the rest of the party, useful when one player is built to tank or recover safely.

items.name: Pity SP
items.how_to_get: Bought from Sketchy Figure or Wealthy Merchant.
items.combat_role: Converts HP into 5 SP, allowing emergency SP recovery when a build needs one more action to heal, revive, or defend.

items.name: SP Wire
items.how_to_get: Bought from Gene or Wealthy Merchant.
items.combat_role: Transfers 5 SP from the user to another party member, useful in coordinated teams where one player carries key actions.

items.name: Launcher
items.how_to_get: Bought from Tai or Banished Knight.
items.combat_role: Replaces Ball with an explosive area attack that can apply Defense Down, trading simplicity for stronger multi-enemy pressure.

items.name: Slingshot
items.how_to_get: Bought from Tai or Banished Knight.
items.combat_role: Replaces Sword with a ranged multi-pellet attack that gains damage but struggles against enemies with defense unless supported by other cards.

items.name: Bombardment
items.how_to_get: Bought from Valor.
items.combat_role: Hits all enemies and pierces up to 2 defense, but failing the action command leaves the user Exhausted.

items.name: Hitmarker
items.how_to_get: Bought from Banished Knight.
items.combat_role: Adds an Ankh effect to the Launcher gameplan, helping set up safer damage cycles against high-pressure enemies.

items.name: Defend
items.how_to_get: Available as a strategy action.
items.combat_role: Restores SP, improves guard timing, and adds hidden damage reduction for the turn, making it a core survival action.

items.name: Focus
items.how_to_get: Available as a strategy action.
items.combat_role: Builds Sword Energy for special attacks, with extra value when combined with Deep Focus.

items.name: Knight
items.how_to_get: Bought from Wealthy Merchant in Vermillion Village.
items.combat_role: Adds Sword or weapon-replacement damage while disabling the Ball slot, making it a core card for melee-focused builds.

items.name: Baller
items.how_to_get: Bought from Wealthy Merchant.
items.combat_role: Adds Ball or weapon-replacement damage while disabling the Sword slot, making it a core card for ranged-focused builds.

items.name: Aggressor
items.how_to_get: Received from Finn McCool after defeating him.
items.combat_role: Adds damage but disables rolling HP, making it powerful for confident players and risky in very hard content.

items.name: Ante Up
items.how_to_get: Bought from Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Adds damage while shrinking timing windows and making failed attacks deal no damage, rewarding clean action-command play.

items.name: Defend+
items.how_to_get: Found on Pit Floor 50 or bought from Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Adds defense, helping stabilize Hard Mode, The Pit, and boss fights with heavy incoming damage.

items.name: Happy SP
items.how_to_get: Found in Rugged Rainforest and Red Sun Temple.
items.combat_role: Generates SP over time, making it efficient in long fights, boss fights, and support builds.

items.name: HP Drain
items.how_to_get: Bought from Amber or Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Restores HP on successful hits while reducing damage, supporting attrition builds that value survival over burst.

items.name: SP Saver
items.how_to_get: Bought from Sketchy Figure.
items.combat_role: Reduces SP card costs by 1 without lowering costs below 1, making action-heavy builds much easier to sustain.

items.name: Hard Mode
items.how_to_get: Given at the start.
items.combat_role: Increases challenge by doubling damage taken and reducing guard windows while increasing XP gain.

cardTierList.title: Block Tales Card Tier List
cardTierList.subtitle: Compare the best Block Tales cards by real use case: beginner safety, boss fights, The Pit, multiplayer support, Knight builds, and Baller builds.
cardTierList.intro: Block Tales cards are strongest when matched to the fight and the build. Damage cards such as Knight, Baller, Ante Up, and Aggressor shine in offense-focused setups, while Happy SP, Defend+, Resurrect, Cure, and Charge DEF are stronger when the goal is survival, support, or long boss fights.

items.summary: Cards that solve common problems across many builds and stay useful in long fights, boss fights, or team play.

cards.name: Happy SP
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Low BP cost and passive SP generation make it useful whenever a fight lasts long enough for extra SP to matter.

cards.name: SP Saver
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Reduces SP costs for card actions, helping Cure, Resurrect, status cards, and defensive actions stay online.

cards.name: Defend+
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Extra defense is one of the cleanest ways to survive high-pressure fights where missed guards can end a run.

cards.name: Resurrect
cards.why_it_ranks_here: A revive option prevents one mistake from ending a party fight and also gives the user a self-revive trigger when SP is available.

cards.name: Charge DEF
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Halved Damage for multiple turns is strong, cheap protection when a player expects a dangerous attack pattern.

items.summary: Cards that create strong offensive builds but depend heavily on action-command skill, weapon choice, and risk tolerance.

cards.name: Knight
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Adds Sword-side damage while locking out Ball, making it one of the simplest ways to build around melee offense.

cards.name: Baller
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Adds Ball-side damage while locking out Sword, fitting players who prefer ranged targeting and Ball-based attacks.

cards.name: Ante Up
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Adds damage but punishes failed timing with zero damage, so it is best when the player can consistently hit commands.

cards.name: Aggressor
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Adds damage but removes rolling HP, which makes it powerful in controlled fights and dangerous in very hard content.

cards.name: Charge
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Adds a strong next-hit attack boost and fits many offensive turns, especially when stacked with weapon damage passives.

items.summary: Cards that become stronger when a battle lasts many turns or when a run needs consistent recovery instead of short burst.

cards.name: SP Regen+
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Improves SP gained from defensive play, giving tankier builds more fuel for expensive actions.

cards.name: HP Drain
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Trades damage for healing on successful hits, which is valuable when survival matters more than burst.

cards.name: SP Drain
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Restores SP on successful hits while lowering damage, making it a utility choice for players who need more card actions.

cards.name: Safe Guard
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Makes guarding more effective, which matters when damage reduction is more valuable than raw attack.

cards.name: Deep Focus
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Improves Focus turns and supports builds that want to reach Sword Energy attacks more reliably.

items.summary: Cards that become much stronger in multiplayer because they protect, heal, revive, or feed resources to teammates.

cards.name: Cure
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Reliable healing keeps allies alive, especially when one teammate is carrying damage or utility.

cards.name: Bodyguard
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Lets one player absorb part of an ally's incoming damage, enabling planned tank-and-support play.

cards.name: Good Vibes
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Turns the user into a recovery engine while the rest of the team keeps fighting.

cards.name: SP Wire
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Moves SP to the teammate who needs it most, which is valuable when one player holds Cure, Resurrect, or a key attack card.

cards.name: Sacrifice
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Converts the user's HP into healing for the rest of the party, making it a strong emergency support option.

items.summary: Cards that are excellent in specific fights, enemy formations, or weapon builds rather than universally best.

cards.name: Feel Fine
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Strong when status effects are the main threat, but less urgent in fights that only test damage and guarding.

cards.name: Daze
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Useful for control and splash, especially when lowering enemy pressure matters.

cards.name: Minimize
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Best when lowering an enemy's threat buys enough time to heal, set up, or finish another target.

cards.name: Cannonball
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Lets Ball hit dangerous contact enemies without popping, which is important in specific matchups.

cards.name: Bombardment
cards.why_it_ranks_here: Excellent for area damage when the player can manage Launcher timing and the Exhausted penalty risk.

bossGuide.title: Block Tales Boss Guide
bossGuide.subtitle: Track the major Block Tales bosses by chapter, location, mechanic, reward, and recommended card approach.
bossGuide.intro: Block Tales boss fights often test more than damage. Good guarding, SP management, status control, defensive turns, and the right card package can matter more than a simple attack build, especially in Hard Mode, optional superbosses, and Chapter 5.

items.name: Supreme Ant
core_mechanics: Summons Ant Armies during the fight.
Punishes players who ignore small enemies for too long.
items.strategy_focus: Clear summons quickly, keep enough SP for defensive turns, and use clean action commands to avoid spending too many recovery items early.

items.name: Sentient Statue
core_mechanics: Appears before the Cruel King fight line.
Tests guard timing and basic resource management.
items.strategy_focus: Treat it as a guard-check fight: defend on dangerous turns, conserve SP, and avoid entering Cruel King with a weak item supply.

items.name: Cruel King
items.location: Blackrock Castle throne room; Cold Nirvana rematch
core_mechanics: Uses Scepter Strike, Freeze, Refreshing Heal, and Call for Help.
Can summon Loyal Knight and Crossbow Crony allies.
Has a no-damage badge challenge through Stylish.
items.strategy_focus: Control summoned enemies before they snowball, guard Freeze turns carefully, and keep SP available for healing or defense instead of spending every turn on damage.

items.name: Supreme Mosquito
items.location: Below the Ant Hill exit after backtracking with the Iron Sword
core_mechanics: Optional backtrack boss tied to a hidden route.
Rewards an economy-focused passive card after defeat.
items.strategy_focus: Enter with a stable defensive setup, use Defend to protect SP flow, and prioritize survival over risky burst because the reward is optional but valuable.

items.name: Bubonic Plant
core_mechanics: Closes the Chapter 2 Griefer route.
A key fight for players moving from early-game fundamentals into longer boss patterns.
items.strategy_focus: Keep a balanced setup: one or two defensive options, a reliable heal, and enough SP generation to avoid running dry before the end of the fight.

items.name: Hatred
core_mechanics: Has Double Turns.
Uses Corruption, PK Thunder, Charge Outburst, and Call for Help.
Can pressure players through repeated high-threat turns.
items.strategy_focus: Plan for multiple attacks per cycle, use defensive cards before burst windows, and keep revive or healing access ready before HP starts rolling down.

items.name: Mutant
items.location: Telamon Manor Red Room and basement route
core_mechanics: Optional superboss connected to Telamon Manor progression.
Defeating it unlocks access to later reward space.
items.strategy_focus: Bring a defensive loadout first, then add damage once the fight's timing is comfortable; optional superbosses punish underprepared glass-cannon builds.

items.name: The Ancients
core_mechanics: Uses Wheel, Beam, Fireball, Ultra Beam, and Call for Help.
Summoned Vessels must be defeated before the boss can be damaged again.
Fireball can apply Burn, and Beam-based attacks can pressure multiple defensive checks.
items.strategy_focus: Clear Vessels fast, protect against status pressure, and save defensive resources for Ultra Beam cycles instead of spending every turn on damage.

items.name: Finn McCool
core_mechanics: Optional superboss that rewards a high-risk damage passive.
Functions as a build check for players who want stronger offensive cards.
items.strategy_focus: Use a stable defensive core first, then add burst tools once guard timing is consistent; the Aggressor reward is strongest after the player can survive without rolling HP.

items.name: The Magnificent

stats.label: Fight rule
stats.value: Ends after several turns when it blows itself up

core_mechanics: The huge HP bar is not meant to be fully depleted normally.
The fight is about surviving the sequence until the scripted finish.
items.strategy_focus: Do not over-invest in burst damage; prioritize survival, SP flow, and safe defensive turns until the encounter resolves.

items.name: The Trinity
core_mechanics: A trio fight that asks players to manage multiple boss threats at once.
Rewards builds that can combine damage, support, and resource control.
items.strategy_focus: Use area or multi-target pressure where possible, keep revive access ready, and avoid letting SP fall too low before the final Chapter 5 stretch.

items.name: Frostmaw
core_mechanics: Final Chapter 5 boss after The Trinity and Seventh Sanctum route.
If Cassie is defeated, the fight is lost.
Commonly pushes players to protect a specific ally instead of only managing personal HP.
items.strategy_focus: Build around ally protection: keep Cassie safe, preserve SP for recovery, and use defensive cards before dangerous turns rather than reacting after damage lands.

badgesGuide.title: Block Tales Badges Guide
badgesGuide.subtitle: Find Block Tales achievements and badges by chapter, including story badges, Hard Mode clears, card collection milestones, hidden badges, and Demo 5 completion goals.
badgesGuide.intro: Block Tales badges reward story progress, hidden interactions, completion milestones, Hard Mode boss clears, card collecting, BUX collecting, challenge clears, and demo participation. Use this section as a 100% completion checklist while moving through each chapter.

items.description: Badges that are not locked to one story chapter and can be tracked across the whole save file.

badges.name: Humble Beginnings
badges.unlock: Start a new save file.

badges.name: Loaded Deck
badges.unlock: Reach 30 BP in one save file.
badges.note: Requires reaching level 10 through BP level-up choices.

badges.name: Card Collector
badges.unlock: Collect 15 unique cards.
badges.note: Part of the card collection milestone line.

badges.name: Card Connoisseur
badges.unlock: Collect 30 unique cards.
badges.note: Continue buying, finding, and earning cards across chapters.

badges.name: Card Hoarder
badges.unlock: Collect 45 unique cards.
badges.note: Requires deeper exploration and shop purchases.

badges.name: Card Enthusiast
badges.unlock: Collect 60 unique cards.
badges.note: Late collection milestone for players tracking all cards.

badges.name: Card Goblin

itemsGuide.title: Block Tales Items Guide
itemsGuide.subtitle: Compare healing foods, SP restorers, attack items, buff items, and key battle consumables before you enter a boss fight.
itemsGuide.intro: Items in Block Tales restore HP or SP, apply buffs, cure status effects, attack enemies, cook recipes, or support overworld progression. This database highlights practical items players can buy, cook, find, fish, or farm from enemies, so you can quickly decide what to keep, sell, or save for hard fights.

items.effect: Restores 5 HP and cures Poison. Using it gives an Apple Core.
items.obtainment: Sold by Tulip, Darren, and Fridge Raider; also drops from Hedgehog, Gaur, and Toxic Spearman at 10.2%.
items.best_use: Safe early-game healing when poison enemies are common.
items.keep_or_spend: Keep a few for poison-heavy routes; buy only when you need cheap recovery.

items.effect: Restores 1 HP and cures Poison.
items.obtainment: Obtained after using an Apple; sold by Tulip; drops from several poison-themed enemies at 20.7%.
items.best_use: Low-cost poison cleanup when HP loss is minor.
items.keep_or_spend: Use freely; it is more of a utility item than a boss-fight heal.

items.effect: Restores 5 HP.
items.obtainment: Sold by Lorenzo, Will, Barley, and Aloe; drops from Ant Army, Devious Rat, Crossbow Crony, Snory Bear, and Hungry Wolf at 6.4%.
items.best_use: Reliable basic healing for early and mid-game routes.
items.keep_or_spend: Keep several as default HP recovery.

items.effect: Restores 8 HP.
items.obtainment: Sold by Lorenzo and Fridge Raider; drops from Spiky Devil and Flaming Horse at 4.4%, and from several toxic enemies at 2.4%.
items.best_use: Better burst healing before bosses or longer chapter routes.
items.keep_or_spend: Save for harder fights instead of using it on small chip damage.

items.effect: Restores 6 HP and becomes a Bitten Brownie after use.
items.obtainment: Cooked from two Bitten Brownies; sold by Will and Eska; drops from Cheeky Eagle and Spiky Killbot at 0.4%.
items.best_use: Efficient two-stage healing because it leaves another smaller heal behind.
items.keep_or_spend: Good to keep when inventory space allows.

items.effect: Restores 5 HP and 15 SP.
items.obtainment: Sold by Will.
items.best_use: Strong sustain item for card-heavy builds that spend SP quickly.
items.keep_or_spend: Save for bosses or The Pit-style endurance fights.

items.effect: Restores 5 SP and grants Fine for 3 turns.
items.obtainment: Sold by Tulip.
items.best_use: Useful before status-heavy fights because Fine helps resist status ailments.
items.keep_or_spend: Buy when your build depends on SP cards.

items.effect: Restores 6 HP and 6 SP, then grants DEF Up LV 2 for 3 turns.
items.obtainment: Cooked from Chickpeas and Pita.
items.best_use: High-value defensive sustain for bosses and dangerous enemy groups.
items.keep_or_spend: Save for hard encounters rather than routine travel.

items.effect: Grants Fine for 3 turns.
items.obtainment: Sold by Barley; drops from Ant Army, Devious Rat, Crossbow Crony, Snory Bear, Hungry Wolf, Banished Knight, Loyal Knight, Supreme Ant, Sentient Statue, and Cruel King at 9.8%.
items.best_use: Prevents status problems during routes with repeated debuffs.
items.keep_or_spend: Keep for status-heavy areas; use before the danger starts.

items.effect: Deals 2 damage to all enemies and also damages the player.
items.obtainment: Sold by Lorenzo and Annihilator; drops from Bee Happy and Winged Zombie at 20.3%, plus many early enemies at lower rates.
items.best_use: Fast area damage when several enemies are alive and your HP can absorb the self-damage.
items.keep_or_spend: Use carefully; avoid when your HP is low.

items.effect: Deals 3 damage to all enemies and also damages the player.
items.obtainment: Cooked from Cherry and Bomb; sold by Fridge Raider; drops from Spiky Devil and Flaming Horse at 12.8%.
items.best_use: Stronger wave-clear option for dangerous multi-enemy battles.
items.keep_or_spend: Save for encounters where ending the fight quickly matters.

items.effect: Deals 2 damage and inflicts Dizzy LV 1 on all enemies and the player.
items.obtainment: Cooked from Dizzy Dial and Bomb; sold by Zuka; drops from Vessel, Vulture, Temple Guardian, The Great Flocci, The Ancients, and several Chapter 4 enemies at around 2.6%-2.8%.
items.best_use: Control option when you need to slow a full enemy group.
items.keep_or_spend: Use when the Dizzy tradeoff is worth also affecting yourself.

items.effect: Grants Burn Enchant LV 2, causing the next attack to inflict Burn LV 2 for 1 turn.
items.obtainment: Found in Manor Forest and Dream Path B; drops from Sharp Nooblet at 3.7% and Smugupine at 3.6%.
items.best_use: Boosts pressure before a high-impact attack.
items.keep_or_spend: Keep for bosses or sturdy enemies that benefit from burn pressure.

items.effect: Revives a fallen ally with 5 HP.
items.obtainment: Drops from Hedgehog, Gaur, and Toxic Spearman at 6%.
items.best_use: Emergency recovery in multiplayer or party-focused encounters.
items.keep_or_spend: Save for boss fights and long challenge runs.

weaponsGuide.title: Block Tales Weapons Guide
weaponsGuide.subtitle: Understand Sword, Ball, replacement weapons, SFOTH swords, and Sword Energy before building your cards around them.
weaponsGuide.intro: Weapons in Block Tales are tied to action cards, overworld abilities, chapter progression, and build identity. Sword and Ball define your base combat options, Slingshot and Launcher replace those slots, and SFOTH swords spend Sword Energy for powerful special effects.

items.name: Sword
items.effect: Hits the first grounded enemy with 1-2 damage and pierces 50% of the target's Defense. Outside combat, it can break barricades, grab close items, and start battles with First Strike LV 1.
items.obtainment: Obtained from Shedletsky after defeating Red Noob and Blue Noob in the Prologue.
items.best_use: Best against grounded enemies with Defense because its partial defense pierce makes it reliable.
items.caution: It skips Flying enemies and can punish the player when hitting Spiky enemies.

items.name: Iron Sword
items.effect: Increases base Sword damage by 1 and allows the player to destroy dense vegetation in the map.
items.obtainment: Obtained after defeating Bigfoot in Mango Tree during Chapter 2.
items.best_use: Important progression upgrade because it improves combat output and unlocks more overworld access.
items.caution: It upgrades Sword directly rather than acting like a separate replacement weapon.

items.name: Ball
items.effect: Deals 1 damage by default. A successful action command adds a second hit that deals 1 damage and ignores Defense.
items.obtainment: Obtained from JParty in the Pre-Prologue when meeting the Mysterious Figure.
items.best_use: Best for targeting any enemy, hitting Flying enemies, grabbing faraway items, and breaking cracked stone pillars.
items.caution: Spiky or Flaming enemies pop the Ball on contact, deny damage, and give the player 1 SP unless Cannonball is equipped.

items.name: Slingshot
items.effect: Replaces Sword with a projectile attack. It can deal more damage than base Sword and can hit Flying and Spiky enemies, but it cannot damage enemies with Defense.
items.obtainment: Bought from Tai for 300 TIX or Banished Knight for 150 TIX.
items.best_use: Good when a fight punishes melee contact or requires reliable hits on Flying and Spiky targets.
items.caution: Avoid it against armored or Defense-heavy enemies.

items.name: Launcher
items.effect: Replaces Ball with a rocket attack that applies DEF Down LV 1 for 3 turns to the target, pierces 1 Defense on the target, and deals splash damage to adjacent enemies.
items.obtainment: Bought from Tai for 300 TIX or Banished Knight for 150 TIX.
items.best_use: Strong against enemy groups because it mixes ranged targeting, debuffing, and splash damage.
items.caution: Missing the timing causes the user to blow up, deal no damage, and gain Exhaustion for 2 turns.

items.name: Ice Dagger
items.effect: Costs 1 Sword Energy and heals the entire party for 20% of max HP and SP, with a minimum of 5 HP and 3 SP.
items.obtainment: Obtained after defeating Cruel King in Chapter 1.
items.best_use: Best sustain tool for long fights because it restores both HP and SP to the party.
items.caution: It uses Sword Energy, so it competes with offensive SFOTH sword options.

items.name: Venomshank
items.effect: Costs 1 Sword Energy, deals 2 damage to all enemies, ignores all Defense, and applies Poison LV 1 for 3 turns.
items.obtainment: Obtained in Chapter 2 after defeating Bubonic Plant and receiving it from Mayor Thaniyel.
items.best_use: Best against groups of enemies that can be poisoned and need steady damage over time.
items.caution: It does not poison enemies that are immune to Poison, such as Komodo Dragon and Bubonic Plant.

items.name: Ghostwalker
items.effect: Costs 2 Sword Energy and applies Dodgy LV 1 to the entire party.
items.obtainment: Obtained in Chapter 3 after fully purifying the player's soul after defeating Hatred.
items.best_use: Best defensive SFOTH sword when survival matters more than burst damage.
items.caution: It costs more Sword Energy than Ice Dagger or Venomshank.

items.name: Firebrand
items.effect: Costs 2 Sword Energy, deals 5 damage to all enemies, ignores all Defense, applies Burn LV 1, and applies ATK Down LV 2 for 3 turns.
items.obtainment: Obtained in Chapter 4 after defeating The Ancients.
items.best_use: Best late-game AOE pressure tool because it combines damage, burn, and enemy attack reduction.
items.caution: It does not benefit from removed-on-strike status effects.

items.name: Sword Energy
items.effect: Starts each battle at 0.5, increases through enemy turns and player attacks, and fuels SFOTH swords. Each SFOTH sword obtained increases the maximum NRG bar by 1.
items.obtainment: Gained naturally in battle, through Focus, NRG Regen, and certain items such as Green Tea, Matcha Dorayaki, Chickpeas, Olives, Kunafa, and Starfruit.
items.best_use: Plan it around boss phases: save NRG for Ice Dagger recovery, Ghostwalker defense, or Firebrand burst.
items.caution: Focus gains less Sword Energy in larger parties, so multiplayer pacing differs from solo play.

statsAndLevelingGuide.title: Block Tales Stats and Leveling Guide
statsAndLevelingGuide.subtitle: Learn what HP, SP, BP, Level Up, respec, and Sword Energy do before choosing your next upgrade.
statsAndLevelingGuide.intro: Block Tales leveling is built around 100 XP level-ups and three main upgrade choices: HP, SP, and BP. HP keeps you alive, SP lets you use more abilities, and BP decides how many cards your build can equip.

items.role: Main progression mechanic triggered after reaching 100 XP.
items.level_up_change: Each level-up lets the player choose +5 HP, +5 SP, or +3 BP.
items.cap_or_start: The level cap starts at 4 and increases by 4 at the end of each chapter, including the Prologue. The maximum level is 24 as of Chapter 5's release.
items.recovery_or_respec: A level-up fully restores the player's HP and SP. Bizville Library can change stat options for 15 TIX per point.
items.build_advice: Pick the stat that fixes your current bottleneck: HP for survival, SP for ability usage, BP for card builds.

items.level_up_change: +5 maximum HP per HP level-up choice.
items.cap_or_start: Players start with 10 HP and can increase it through level-ups or HP+ cards.
items.recovery_or_respec: Recovered by winning battles, food, Cure, Ice Dagger, Healing Pads, Beds, Happy HP, HP Drain, Sacrifice effects, Good Vibes, and Anchor.
items.build_advice: Prioritize HP when learning enemy patterns, fighting bosses, or running builds that take contact damage from melee or self-damaging items.

items.role: Action points used to perform abilities and card moves in battle.
items.level_up_change: +5 maximum SP per SP level-up choice.
items.cap_or_start: Maximum SP from level-ups is 90 without cards.
items.recovery_or_respec: Recovered through winning battles, SP-granting items, Ice Dagger, Healing Pads, Beds, Train Spa hot tub, Happy SP, SP Drain, Pity SP, Defending, Passing, and SP Wire for non-users.
items.build_advice: Prioritize SP when your equipped cards are powerful but you run out of resources before the fight ends.

items.role: Determines how many cards the player can equip.
items.level_up_change: +3 maximum BP per BP level-up choice.
items.recovery_or_respec: Removing equipped cards refunds their BP cost.
items.build_advice: Prioritize BP when new cards unlock your build, especially Knight, Baller, support cards, defensive cards, or SP-saving setups.

items.role: Battle resource used for SFOTH swords such as Ice Dagger, Venomshank, Ghostwalker, and Firebrand.
items.level_up_change: Not a normal level-up choice. Each SFOTH sword obtained increases the NRG bar maximum by 1.
items.cap_or_start: Each battle starts with 0.5 Sword Energy.
items.recovery_or_respec: Gained through enemy turns, player attacks, low-HP attacks, Focus, Deep Focus, NRG Regen, and items such as Green Tea, Matcha Dorayaki, Chickpeas, Olives, Kunafa, and Starfruit.
items.build_advice: Use NRG planning for longer fights: Ice Dagger for recovery, Venomshank for poison pressure, Ghostwalker for defense, and Firebrand for late-game burst.

items.role: Simple upgrade path for players still learning action commands and enemy behavior.
items.level_up_change: Open with HP if you are frequently fainting, then add BP once you find cards worth equipping, and add SP when your card moves start draining resources.
items.cap_or_start: Works well before your build becomes specialized.
items.recovery_or_respec: Can be corrected later through the Bizville Library stat-change option.
items.build_advice: A stable beginner build usually values survival first, then card flexibility, then SP economy.

items.role: Upgrade logic for longer fights where mistakes, SP costs, and defense matter.
items.level_up_change: Mix HP and SP if you need to survive repeated hits while still casting cards. Add BP when defensive or support cards are the main reason your build works.
items.cap_or_start: Best for chapters where bosses punish low HP or long SP droughts.
items.recovery_or_respec: Carry HP/SP items and plan Sword Energy for Ice Dagger or Ghostwalker turns.
items.build_advice: Boss builds should avoid over-investing in only one stat unless the card setup clearly supports that choice.

items.role: Upgrade logic for Knight, Baller, utility, support, or passive-heavy setups.
items.level_up_change: BP unlocks more cards, while SP determines how often active card moves can be used.
items.cap_or_start: BP has a firm 30-point cap; HP and SP can be extended further with cards.
items.recovery_or_respec: Removing cards refunds BP, making experimentation easier than permanent stat choices.
items.build_advice: If your build feels weak because you cannot equip enough cards, choose BP. If you can equip the cards but cannot afford their moves, choose SP.

enemyDropsGuide.title: Block Tales Enemy Drops Guide
enemyDropsGuide.subtitle: Find useful item drops, rare card drops, and farming targets by chapter instead of repeatedly checking every enemy page.
enemyDropsGuide.intro: Enemies in Block Tales can carry listed items or cards during battle. Visible held drops have a chance to drop after defeat, hidden drops can appear under the enemy health bar, and each enemy can only hold one item or card at a time.

items.notable_drops: Enemies may carry items or cards from their journal entries. A visible held item or card has a 50% drop chance; an item displayed under the enemy health bar is guaranteed.
items.best_for: Understanding why farming works differently when an item is visible, hidden, or not shown.
items.farm_note: Each enemy can only hold one item or card. If an enemy visibly carries something, it cannot also carry a hidden item.

items.farming_target: Nooblet / Helmet Noob / Cultist Noob / Knightly Noob / Blue Noob / Red Noob / Noobador
items.notable_drops: Apple Juice 7%, Bomb 7%, Hypno Glasses 3.5%, Tickle Stick 3.5%, Hot Chocolate 2.7%, Minimize 1.6%, Slow Go 1.6%.
items.best_for: Early Bombs, Apple Juice, and first utility-card farming.
items.farm_note: Good early route when you need cheap consumables before moving into later chapters.

items.notable_drops: Bomb 7.4%, Hypno Glasses 3.7%, Dizzy Dial 3.7%, Cheezburger 3.7%, Bloxy Cola 2.9%, ATK-FX R 1.7%, Slow Go 1.7%.
items.best_for: Farming early flying-enemy drops and small recovery items.
items.farm_note: Bring Ball or another ranged option because Sword does not naturally target Flying enemies.

items.notable_drops: Bomb 7.4%, Epicsauce 3.7%, Hypno Glasses 3.7%, Cheezburger 3.7%, Bloxy Cola 2.9%, Slow Go 1.7%, Softener 1.7%.
items.best_for: Early Epicsauce, Softener, and Bomb farming.
items.farm_note: Avoid careless melee hits if the enemy's Spiky trait is active or relevant to your setup.

items.farming_target: Ant Army / Devious Rat / Snory Bear / Hungry Wolf / Banished Knight / Loyal Knight / Crossbow Crony / Sentient Statue / Supreme Ant / Cruel King
items.notable_drops: Worm 19.7%, Hot Chocolate 9.8%, Cheezburger 6.4%, Dizzy Dial 2.5%, Ice Storm 2%, Free Ice 1.2%, Taco 1%, Strawberry Ice Cream 1%.
items.best_for: Hot Chocolate, Cheezburger, and Free Ice farming.
items.farm_note: A strong chapter route for building a basic stock of recovery and cold-themed utility.

items.notable_drops: Forbidden Drink 24.8%, Bitten Brownie 4%, Glue 3.2%, Onion Ring 2.8%, Ice Storm 2.5%, Snowball 1.5%, Vanilla Ice Cream 1.2%, Bloxy Cola 1.2%, Brownie 0.4%.
items.best_for: Snowball card attempts and special Chapter 1 consumables.
items.farm_note: Use anti-Flying and anti-Spiky planning because this group includes both traits.

items.notable_drops: Iron's Choice 5.7%, Bomb 5%, Tickle Stick 4.1%, Epicsauce 3.6%, Hypno Glasses 3.6%, Chocolate Ice Cream 2.9%, ROBAR 2.9%, Storm Tea 0.6%.
items.best_for: Epicsauce, Tickle Stick, and Iron Cafe-style item farming.
items.farm_note: Good target when you want utility items rather than basic HP food.

items.farming_target: Komodo Dragon / Tree Golem / Toxic Dartman / Venom Wizard / Purple Frog / Bigfoot / Griefer / Bubonic Plant
items.notable_drops: Apple Core 20.7%, Dumpling 5.3%, Bomb 4.8%, Green Pepper 3.1%, Dizzy Dial 3.1%, Double Cheezburger 2.4%, Feel Fine 1%, Free Poison 0.7%, Green Tea 0.3%.
items.best_for: Poison-area recovery, Dumpling, Double Cheezburger, and Free Poison farming.
items.farm_note: Carry Poison cures and consider farming Apple Cores here because the drop pool strongly supports poison cleanup.

items.farming_target: Flying Mantis / Mosquito Swarm / Supreme Mosquito
items.notable_drops: Papaya 13.9%, Mango 5.4%, Pineapple 3.9%, Jackfruit 3.7%, Orange 3.7%, Chocolate Ice Cream 2.9%, Tickle Stick 1.2%, Free Poison 1%.
items.best_for: Fruit-based healing and Free Poison farming.
items.farm_note: Use Ball, Sword Toss, Slingshot, or other anti-Flying options to keep farming efficient.

items.notable_drops: Apple 10.2%, Hot Leaf Juice 6%, Red Pepper 5.6%, Lemon 4.7%, Bahulu 4.3%, Cinnamon Roll 2.3%, Thorns 2%, Bomb 1.1%.

items.farm_note: Useful when preparing for harder fights because Hot Leaf Juice can revive a fallen ally.

items.best_for: Summon-card farming and SP-related food routes.
items.farm_note: One of the clearer farming targets because Call: Gorilla is tied directly to Gorilla.

items.farming_target: Bandit / Big Bandit / Helmet Bandit / Purple Noob / Green Noob / Zombie / Skeleton / Squatter / VLAD / Ghost of HAX / Turkey / Slasher / Smiling Bear / Borange Boy / Mold Man / Confident Boulder / F'Ella / Greed / Solitude / Fear / Phobia / Apparition / Hatred
items.notable_drops: Bloxy Cola 22.2%, Mr Batty 5.4%, Applesauce 5.1%, Glue 2.9%, Slateskin Potion 2.3%, Cherry Bomb 2.2%, Orange Juice 1.3%, Pie 0.4%.

items.farm_note: Good mid-game stock-up route for group healing and AOE attack items.

items.notable_drops: Bomb 20.3%, Smoked Trout 4.5%, Cherry 3.9%, Gulab Kulfi 2.6%, HP Finder 1.1%, Cookie Dough 1.1%, First Aid Kit 1.1%, Unadon 1.1%.

items.farm_note: Avoid relying on Ball contact against Flaming or Spiky enemies unless your setup handles those traits.

items.best_for: Rare food, Relic, and special cooking material farming.
items.farm_note: Best for collectors who want unusual drops rather than basic recovery items.

items.notable_drops: Snowball 1.5%, Minimize 1.6%, Fireball 1.2%, Softener 1.7%, Free Ice 1.5%, Free Poison 1%, Free Fire 1.5%, Feel Fine 1%, Call: Gorilla 7.1%, Thorns 1.8%, Slow Go around 1.6%-1.7%, Anchor 1.6%, HP Finder 1.1%.
items.best_for: Players farming specific cards instead of consumable items.
items.farm_note: Farm the enemy group tied to the card instead of farming a whole chapter blindly.

locationsAndMapGuide.title: Block Tales Locations and Map Guide
locationsAndMapGuide.subtitle: Find the main hubs, chapter routes, shops, card vendors, boss entrances, and travel connections across Block Tales.
locationsAndMapGuide.intro: Use this Block Tales map guide as a route index: start from Bizville, move through Roblox HQ, follow each chapter area in order, and jump to the place tied to the card, item, boss, or story objective you need.

items.name: Bizville
items.how_to_access: Loaded when a new save file starts, then revisited between chapters.
main_uses: Lorenzo's Shop for HP and SP recovery items
The Library for stat respecs
Alleyway card purchases from Sketchy Figure during the Prologue
The Docks route to Vermillion Village after Chapter 3
The Sewers entrance to The Pit of 100 Trials

items.name: Roblox HQ and Bizville Meadows
items.region_type: Story hub and early combat route
items.how_to_access: Head through East Bizville into Bizville Meadows, then enter Roblox HQ near the Meadows entrance.
main_uses: Progress the early story after the VIP Convention setup
Fight Red Noob and Blue Noob inside Roblox HQ
Use the Meadows route toward the Caves
Visit the Fishing Spot for fish items

items.name: Blackrock Castle
items.region_type: Castle dungeon and first SFOTH sword finale
items.how_to_access: Progress from the Caves through Roadtown, Mount Blackrock, and Snowy Thicket.
main_uses: Solve the prison-cell code route
Get the Blackrock Diner Key and access the castle kitchen path
Pick up the Shrinking card in the Blackrock Kitchen route
Reach the throne room for the Cruel King fight
Obtain the Ice Dagger after Chapter One

items.name: Rugged Rainforest and Sacred Hollow
items.how_to_access: Use the West Bizville Airport route to Plainstown, then continue through Savannah, Turitopulis, and the rainforest.
main_uses: Fight the Komodo Dragon and receive Rocket Boots
Use Coconut Water Machine interactions
Follow Griefer encounters through the rainforest
Reach Mango Tree, Sacred Hollow, and Griefer's Crib
Obtain the Venomshank after defeating Bubonic Plant

items.name: Bizville Railways, Spooksville, and Telamon's Manor
items.region_type: Train route, haunted manor, and Ghostwalker route
items.how_to_access: Receive the Train Ticket from Shedletsky, then board from Bizville Railways in West Bizville.
main_uses: Clear the invaded train route
Reach Spooksville and the cemetery path
Enter Telamon's Manor and its haunted version
Complete Ghost Chef ingredient objectives
Descend into the Catacombs and trigger the Dream World route

items.name: Dream World
items.how_to_access: Touch the Ghostwalker in the Catacombs, then progress through the player's mind.
main_uses: Move through The House, The Path, and Dream World Hub
Clear Greed, Solitude, and Fear paths
Use Ghost Potion timing in the red-tinted Hatred route
Defeat Hatred and purify the player's soul
Obtain the Ghostwalker after Chapter Three

items.name: Scorched Dunes and Temple of the Red Sun
items.region_type: Desert route, puzzle temple, and Firebrand route
items.how_to_access: Use Bizville Docks after Chapter Three, then travel by boat to the Scorched Dunes.
main_uses: Clear desert enemies and cave-style puzzle sections
Defeat The Great Flocci and obtain the Bottled Ship
Visit Vermillion Village for cards and items
Solve four Scorched Ruins puzzles to open the Temple of the Red Sun
Use temple orb, sequence, and mirror puzzles before the Firebrand finale

items.name: The Heights
items.chapter: Chapter Five: Trouble on the Heights
items.region_type: Demo 5 sky route and Windforce chapter route
items.how_to_access: Use the West Bizville In the Cards building route and helicopter-pad path unlocked for Chapter Five progression.
main_uses: Enter the Demo 5 chapter route
Clear the Wizard Tower boss path
Travel through Redcliff toward Reginald
Challenge the Trinity in Trinity Castle
Complete the Seventh Sanctum and fight Frostmaw for the Windforce finale

pitOf100TrialsGuide.title: Block Tales Pit of 100 Trials Guide
pitOf100TrialsGuide.subtitle: Plan your Pit run by floor range, enemy theme, TIX payout, checkpoint floors, and card rewards.
pitOf100TrialsGuide.intro: The Block Tales Pit of 100 Trials is entered through the Bizville Sewers after obtaining Shrinking from Blackrock Castle. Enemy sets escalate by chapter theme, and every tenth floor acts as a reward floor with a Healing Pad, spawn point, and card reward.

items.safe_floor_reward: Bed and spawn point on Floor 0
recommended_prep: Bring HP and SP recovery before entering
Equip a simple survival build before pushing beyond the first checkpoint
Use Shrinking to enter the tunnel toward the sewers

items.safe_floor_reward: Floor 10: Healing Pad, spawn point, Daze card
recommended_prep: Use this range for early TIX practice
Prioritize consistent blocks and basic HP sustain
Treat Floor 9 as the first boss-check floor

items.safe_floor_reward: Floor 20: Healing Pad, spawn point, Free Ice card
recommended_prep: Prepare for higher incoming damage from castle and mountain enemies
Bring multi-target options for grouped enemies
Use bombs or strong single-target damage when bulky enemies appear

items.safe_floor_reward: Floor 30: Healing Pad, spawn point, Thorns card
recommended_prep: Carry healing for poison-heavy jungle waves
Use defensive cards before Floor 29 Komodo Dragon pressure
Keep enough SP for enemies that punish slow single-target clears

items.theme: Chapter Three and Dream World enemies
items.safe_floor_reward: Floor 40: Healing Pad, spawn point, Deep Focus card
recommended_prep: Bring status control and SP recovery
Expect mixed physical and spooky enemy patterns
Save resources for Floors 38-39 where emotion bosses appear

items.theme: Chapter Four desert and temple enemies
items.safe_floor_reward: Floor 50: Healing Pad, spawn point, Defend+ card
recommended_prep: Use fire-resistant routing and strong guard timing
Reserve burst damage for Floor 49 Captain Trotter lineup
Defensive builds gain strong value from the Defend+ reward

items.safe_floor_reward: Floor 60: Healing Pad, spawn point, Pack Tactics card
recommended_prep: Bring a late-game survival build before pushing this range
Plan around mixed Chapter Five enemies and returning boss threats
Keep ally, defense, and SP tools ready for the final stretch

updatesAndPatchNotes.title: Block Tales Updates and Patch Notes
updatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Track major Block Tales releases, Demo 5 changes, new chapters, Pit expansions, cards, items, balance passes, and official game status.
updatesAndPatchNotes.intro: This Block Tales update hub is built for release-date, patch-notes, and next-demo search intent. Each entry focuses on what changed for players: new story content, new floor ranges, new cards and items, balance updates, UI changes, and progression features.

highlights: Chapter Five: Trouble on the Heights released

Pre-Prologue added to the main story campaign
Chapter Four added to the main story campaign
Aggressor, Infect, and Free Fire cards added

Vermillion Village superboss route added after Chapter 4

Allies added to assist in battle
Cooking added through the Kitchen Wizard

Rolling health meter speed reduced when not thinking
Item drop rates became visible through Enemy Index
Several card and item effects adjusted

Final boss refights added near the airport route
Slingshot and Launcher weapons added in Turitopulis
Five difficulty cards added in Bizville

balance_changes: Cards became sellable to vendors for TIX

Prologue and Chapter One route established

storyAndLoreRecap.title: Block Tales Story and Lore Recap
storyAndLoreRecap.subtitle: Catch up on the main story, SFOTH sword hunt, chapter order, key characters, and major turning points before starting the newest demo.
storyAndLoreRecap.intro: Block Tales follows the player after Builderman's kidnapping and Shedletsky's request to recover the Sword Fight on the Heights swords. Each major chapter sends the player into a new region, ends with a major boss confrontation, and adds a new storyline sword to the player's arsenal.

items.heading: Pre-Prologue: The Roblox VIP Convention
items.summary: The story opens at the Roblox VIP Convention in Roblox HQ, where Builderman gives a speech before the Mysterious Figure interrupts, criticizes modern Roblox, and orders his minions to tie Builderman up. The player is pushed into the first fight when the Elder Penguin asks them to stand up and use the Superball.

items.heading: Prologue: Hello World!
items.summary: The player wakes up in Tutorial Terry's bed and learns that Builderman has been kidnapped. After reaching Roblox HQ, the player finds Shedletsky injured, defeats Red Noob and Blue Noob, receives a wooden sword, and follows the Meadows route toward Noobador.

items.heading: Chapter One: Storming the Castle
items.summary: The player climbs through the Caves and reaches Roadtown, a town trapped in unnatural winter. The journey continues through Snowy Thicket and into Blackrock Castle, ending with the Cruel King using the Ice Dagger as the chapter's final boss weapon.

items.heading: Chapter Two: A Toxic Time
items.summary: After the Ice Dagger is recovered, Shedletsky sends the player toward Plainstown and the Rugged Rainforest to retrieve the Venomshank. The chapter centers on Mayor Thaniyel, Griefer, jungle traversal, Rocket Boots, Sacred Hollow, and Griefer's transformation into Bubonic Plant.

items.heading: Chapter Three: All Hallows' Eve
items.summary: Shedletsky sends the player to Telamon's Manor to recover the Ghostwalker. The train to Spooksville is invaded, the manor becomes haunted, the Catacombs lead to Ghostwalker, and touching it sends the player into Dream World to face Greed, Solitude, Fear, and Hatred.

items.heading: Chapter Four: Excavation Exploration
items.summary: After the Ghostwalker is secured, Shedletsky sends the player through Bizville Docks to the Scorched Dunes to retrieve the Firebrand from the Temple of the Red Sun. The route introduces The Great Flocci, Captain Trotter, Calypso, desert puzzles, temple guardians, and the Firebrand finale involving The Ancients.

items.heading: Chapter Five: Trouble on the Heights
items.summary: Chapter Five sends the player into the Heights route introduced with Demo 5. The progression path includes Wizard Tower, Redcliff, Reginald's Throne, Trinity Castle, the Seventh Sanctum, and Frostmaw as the final Windforce boss encounter.

items.heading: SFOTH Sword Order
items.summary: The main sword progression follows the chapter reward structure: Ice Dagger after Chapter One, Venomshank after Chapter Two, Ghostwalker after Chapter Three, Firebrand after Chapter Four, and Windforce after Chapter Five. Sword Energy, also called NRG, is the battle resource used for SFOTH sword actions.
items.sword_reward: Ice Dagger, Venomshank, Ghostwalker, Firebrand, Windforce

questions.question: What is Block Tales?
questions.answer: Block Tales is a Roblox turn-based RPG by Spaceman Moonbase with chapter routes, card builds, bosses, badges, BUX, and story updates.

questions.question: Does Block Tales have codes?
questions.answer: The Block Tales Codes module tracks the main RPG reward status and separates it from similarly named spin-off code lists.

questions.question: What should new Block Tales players learn first?
questions.answer: New Block Tales players should learn HP, SP, BP, cards, blocking, level-up choices, and the Prologue route before pushing deep into later chapters.

questions.question: What changed in Block Tales Demo 5?
questions.answer: Block Tales Demo 5 added Chapter Five, Trouble on the Heights, Windforce progression, new enemies, new items, new cards, The Pit floors, and rewrite changes.
